item.title: Air France-KLM kicks off bidding race for Portugal’s TAP
item.description: Franco-Dutch group first to submit non-binding offer for up to 49.9% of carrier

item.description: Oil traders that normally thrive on volatility caught out by scale of crisis as energy shock unfurled

item.title: Gulf states consider new pipelines to avoid Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Conflict prompts countries to revisit plans that replicate Saudi Arabia’s East-West pipeline despite huge cost and complexity

item.description: Middle East conflict holds out promise to bring another windfall for defence groups as governments restock arsenals

item.description: AI start-up held talks with Google and other cloud providers in bid to revive ambitious 2-gigawatt Texas project

item.description: Tricky Dick’s trade and currency war gave way to a pivot towards the multilateral Alan Beattie

item.description: Governments from Bangladesh to Zambia impose measures to cut fuel demand as Middle East conflict cuts off flows

item.title: UK bank capital rules are becoming a drag on growth
item.description: Reforming the capital regime would unlock meaningful lending capacity and strengthen Britain’s competitiveness

item.description: Controversial US tech group was awarded a £330mn contract in 2023 to collate hospital and patient information

item.description: Japanese industry and Chinese manufacturing prowess played roles in the tech giant’s success, with consequences for both

item.description: Quinn Slobodian and Ben Tarnoff pinpoint the SpaceX entrepreneur’s clever leveraging of state support to exert influence over government itself

item.description: CEO Guillaume Faury sees opportunities in helicopter manufacturing as well as transport planes, tankers, and space equipment

item.description: Primetime TV address signals escalation rather than a swift peace deal

item.description: Levies of 100% on certain medicines would follow through on threats made by Trump made last year

item.description: Discussions will include American and international insurance watchdogs

item.description: Péladeau says Quebecor paid ‘a bit too much’ for the rights in the 2013 deal but declines to provide target that would make sense for company

item.description: Artemis II overcomes technical delays to launch smoothly from Kennedy Space Center

item.description: Airline is interviewing CEO candidates after announcing that Rousseau will retire at the end of September

item.description: The most competitive offers are still found in promotional savings accounts and short-term GICs

item.title: Health Canada imposes new conditions on Grifols after deaths of two people who donated plasma
item.description: Regulator says Grifols centres must reduce number of appointments, better supervise new staff and improve donor records

item.description: Conflict disrupts shopping across sportswear retailer’s Europe, Middle East and Africa business, adding to its struggles in China

item.description: Potential investment with Leapmotor would be first from Chinese manufacturer since Carney reduced tariffs on country’s EVs in January

item.description: Montreal-based retailer reports fourth-quarter profits more than doubled from last year as it expands beyond North America

item.description: Investment chief at US International Development Finance Corporation says agreement includes offtake controls

item.description: Regulator agreed to exempt carriers from cash penalties if flight disruptions arose from mechanical issues or labour strikes

item.description: Production declined and new orders dropped as U.S. tariffs weigh on critical sectors

item.description: Low fees, better apps and fractional shares have made investing easier than ever

item.description: Last time drivers collectively paid this much at the pump was after Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine

item.title: At long last, more Canadians are waiting until 70 to start their CPP pensions
item.description: Until recently, very few retirees postponed the start of their CPP pensions beyond age 65, even if waiting means they receive much more

item.description: Good harvests and plentiful grain stocks will provide some cushion against the impact of the Iran war Alan Beattie

item.description: A series of uncannily timed bets on the price of oil and stocks deserves a proper investigation. It’s far from clear that they’ll get one.
